name: Biome Generation
description: Biome Generation is a content-generation mechanic that creates spaces, events, rewards, or structures from rules and seeds.
design_notes: Biome Generation should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For procedural generation mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use biome generation as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect biome generation to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels each run or world can surprise them while still following understandable rules.

parameters.name: seed_value
parameters.description: Seed used to make generation reproducible.

parameters.name: generation_depth
parameters.description: Number of passes used to construct and validate output.

parameters.name: content_budget
parameters.description: Maximum number of pieces, rooms, encounters, or rewards generated.

edge_cases: Biome Generation is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Biome Generation overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Biome Generation runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Biome Generation state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Biome Generation plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Biome Generation produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Biome Generation strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Biome Generation becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Biome Generation with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Biome Generation as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Biome Generation with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Biome Generation as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
